blog_title: Top 5 Breathtaking Places You Must Visit In Leh Ladakh
seo_title: Top 5 Breathtaking Places You Must Visit In Leh Ladakh
description: Discover the ultimate travel packing list for every adventure. Pack smart, light, and stress-free with essential tips .
body_content:
Published by travelxploria on Tue Jun 17 2025

No words would be enough to describe the serenity and beauty of Leh Ladakh. All those videos and pictures that you find on the internet would be nothing in comparison to the experience that you can get upon visiting there.
The soul-soothing place, situated at an altitude of 8,370 ft from sea level, offers the Himalayan range a splendid mix of white and green. The ruggedness of the Himalayas can be witnessed with wide expanses of white sand. However, the peaceful vibe that the lake offers is the best. Once you visit there, you will be lost in its breathtaking beauty.

If you are already making plans to go there, then remember, the best season to visit is the summer. A large number of tourist places are open during the months of April and May. To avoid excessive crowd, some people prefer visiting during the June-August months. In order to give a better shape to your plans, get in touch with a travel agency looking for the best Leh Ladakh tour package from Kolkata.

Going to Leh Ladakh, make sure you stop by these 5 beautiful places:

Tso Moriri’s enchanting experience:

Similar in astounding beauty and bluish color, Tso Moriri is like the alter ego of Pangong. If you want to look at a famous artist’s oil painting live that captures brownish mountains towering over the Moriri on one side and lush green countryside on the other, then do visit it once. Right from geese to seagulls to black-neck cranes, as the lake attracts quite a number of migratory birds, it’s another reason to go there if you are a passionate bird-watcher. Want to truly feel and absorb the scenes? Make sure you camp at the Korzok village in the vicinity of the lake. It will be more enthralling than you can imagine!

Nubra Valley’s folk-tales-like aura:

While planning a trip to Leh Ladakh, a desert is probably the last place that you will expect but there is one. The mesmerizing sand dunes of the Nubra Valley provide an Arabian-Nights-like experience to all tourists. If you are interested in animals then you will be delighted to have a riding experience of the famous double-humped camels found in this region. It’s bound to be the highlight of your trip. The beauty of this place lies in the fact that it lets you look over the mountain regions of the Zanskar Valley.

Desolate yet thrilling Zanskar Valley:

Covering an area of 7000 sq km, the Zanskar mountain range is without a doubt, one of the most barren places in the Himalayan range. The tributaries of the Zanskar River are well-known for seasonal white-water rafting. The trek over the Zanskar River is very famous and is often undertaken by adventure enthusiasts in huge numbers. Usually, people go Chadar trek in the month of November when the river is frozen and safe to trek.

Pangong Tso – Straight from the movie!

Have you watched 3 Idiots? Remember the place at the climax scene where Chatur realizes Rancho’s true identity? That enchanting lake is called Pangong Tso. You need to undertake a 160 km journey from Leh to visit this lake which is the world’s highest brackish lake at 14,256 feet. You will forget the blue oceans you have seen, once you arrive at the Pangong Lake and witness the wonderful blue shade.

The tribe of Dah Hanu:

Dah and Hanu are the names of the two villages. They are a few among those villages where the people of the Brokpa tribe live. If you are someone who would love to get to know them then it’s an opportunity you shouldn’t miss.

So if Leh Ladakh is already on your travel bucket list and you are spreading your arms to welcome Nature and its breathtaking beauty, contact a top-rated travel agency that can offer you the best assistance when it comes to planning and executing the trip.
